stem: A factory produces microchips using two sources. Fabrication Line A handles 78% of production with a known defect rate of 3.9%. Fabrication Line B handles 22% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 4.0% and 7.4%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected microchip is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Fabrication Line B?

ground_truth.answer: P(Fabrication Line B | defective) ranges from 0.224 to 0.349 as Fabrication Line B's defect rate varies from 4.0% to 7.4%. Point estimate at 5.7%: 0.292.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.224, 0.349] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
